title: aluta appressa
intro: Aluta appressa, also known as the tick trefoil, is a type of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Aluta appressa has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
